channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The "Garden Variety" podcast offers tips and tricks for plant parents of all levels
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Charity Nebbe and Aaron Steil, hosts of Iowa Public Radio's Garden Variety podcast.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Charity Nebbe and Aaron Steil, hosts of Iowa Public Radio's Garden Variety podcast.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: An Iranian-American writer describes the "vertigo" he feels about the war
item.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.
item.content:encoded: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Fab 5 Freddy recalls how he brought underground art forms mainstream respect
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Fab 5 Freddy, a pioneer of graffiti art and hip-hop filmmaking, on his new memoir Everybody's Fly.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Fab 5 Freddy, a pioneer of graffiti art and hip-hop filmmaking, on his new memoir Everybody's Fly.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: In Indonesia, a fishing village replants mangrove forests one seedling at a time
item.description: Over the last 30 years, 40% of Indonesia's mangroves have been cut down mainly to create fish and shrimp farms, destroying local ecosystems. But one small indigenous fishing village is changing that.
item.content:encoded: Over the last 30 years, 40% of Indonesia's mangroves have been cut down mainly to create fish and shrimp farms, destroying local ecosystems. But one small indigenous fishing village is changing that.
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: Spain offers a different approach to dealing with undocumented migrants
item.description: Spain plans to give half a million undocumented migrants legal status.
item.content:encoded: Spain plans to give half a million undocumented migrants legal status.
item.dc:creator: Alexis T. Marshall

item.title: NPR critics revisit the ghosts of Oscars past
item.description: The Oscars are tonight, and not everyone will be happy with who wins and loses. But what if we had a chance to pick the Oscars? This week, we're looking into Oscars past and relitigating some wins.
item.content:encoded: The Oscars are tonight, and not everyone will be happy with who wins and loses. But what if we had a chance to pick the Oscars? This week, we're looking into Oscars past and relitigating some wins.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: In a small Lebanese town, grief and fear follow the Michigan synagogue attack
item.description: The suspect who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon on March 5. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon on March 5. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.

(Image credit: JOSEPH EID)
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: President Trump orders a Marine rapid response force to the Middle East
item.description: NPR's Jane Arraf reports on developments in the war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Jane Arraf reports on developments in the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: A Game Challenges Californians To Find A Working Payphone
item.description: When was the last time you saw a payphone? For most people it's probably been a while. In California, there are more than 2000 working payphones. A game challenges the public to find them.
item.content:encoded: When was the last time you saw a payphone? For most people it's probably been a while. In California, there are more than 2000 working payphones. A game challenges the public to find them.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Acovino

item.title: Fixing the toxic work culture of restaurants
item.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with restaurant industry veterans Kenji Lopez-Alt and Hannah Selinger about ways to fix the toxic work culture in many restaurant kitchens.
item.content:encoded: Host Adrian Ma speaks with restaurant industry veterans Kenji Lopez-Alt and Hannah Selinger about ways to fix the toxic work culture in many restaurant kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma
